rulings.Case Title: WEST BAY BUILDERS, INC. VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Demurrer To West Bay'S Complaint; Or In The Alternative, Request For Stay; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 3, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Demurrer To West Bay's Complaint; Or In The Alternative, Request For Stay; Request For Judicial Notice. DEMURRER OVERRULED, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. THE COURT DENIES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF 4/23/08 LETTER. SEE: CRUZ V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 1131. REQUEST FOR STAY DENIED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PORFERIO EBORLAS VS. INDYMAC BANK, FSB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Plaintiff: Porferio Eborlas)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 3, 2010, line 6, Plaintiff Porferio Eborlas Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE CONSISTS OF ONE SPARSE DECLARATION AND A VERIFIED COMPLAINT THAT IS BARE OF FACTS AND CONSISTS LARGELY OF LEGAL CONCLUSIONS AND IMPERMISSIBLE OPINIONS. A MORTGAGE FOR REAL PROPERTY IS NOT SUBJECT TO ANY TRANSLATION REQUIREMENT UNDER CC SECTION 1632 EXCEPT FOR A PARTICULAR TYPE, SUCH AS A REVERSE MORTGAGE. THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT REGARDING THE LANGUAGE BARRIER IS INCONSISTENT AND NOT CONVINCING. CC SECTION 2923.6 DOES NOT REQUIRE DEFENDANTS TO ENTER INTO A LOAN MODIFICATION AND DOES NOT PREVENT FORECLOSURE. CC SECTION 2924 ET SEQ. IS A COMPREHENSIVE SCHEME REGARDING FORECLOSURES AND DOES NOT MANDATE THAT THE NOTE BE PRODUCED. NO EVIDENCE IS PROVIDED REGARDING DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS FOR NOTICE OF DEFAULT PROVISIONS OF CC SECTION 2924 AND NOTICE OF SALE PROVISIONS OF CC SECTION 2924(f). NO EVIDENCE OR AUTHORITY IS PROVIDED SHOWING THAT THE FAILURE TO DISCLOSE ALL OF THE LOAN DOCUMENTS WOULD RENDER THE DEED OF TRUST VOID RATHER THAN SIMPLY VOIDABLE. NO TENDER WAS OFFERED OR ACCOMPLISHED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO MAKE PAYMENTS ON THE NOTE AND THE DELAYS RESULTING FROM SUCCESSIVE BANKRUPTCY FILINGS RESULT IN UNCLEAN HANDS AND LACHES. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DANA STREET LLC A NEVADA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. SCOTT MORROW et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 3, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT SCOTT MORROW'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF, AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF OFFERS NO EVIDENCE TO CONTROVERT THE EVIDENCE OFFERED BY DEFENDANT IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT COMPLIED WITH THE ELLIS ACT AND THE SF RENT ORDINANCE SECTION 37.9(a)(13) THAT REQUIRE IT TO GO OUT OF THE RENTAL BUSINESS BY EVICTING THEIR TENANTS AND WITHDRAWING ALL UNITS FROM THE RENTAL MARKET. UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT UNITS IN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY HAVE BEEN RENTED SINCE THE NOTICE OF TERMINATION OF TENANCY. SEE THE DECLARATIONS OF SALAS, LOPEZ, STUTZ, SEGURA, AND CARTAGENA. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT CARTAGENA WAS ACTING AS AN AGENT FOR PLAINTIFF AS A MATTER OF LAW WHEN HE ENTERED INTO RENTAL AGREEMENTS AFTER THE DATE PLAINTIFF TERMINATED TENANCY AT THE SUBJECT PREMISES. (CARTAGENA DECLARATION AND ATTACHED EXHIBITS.) =(302/CWW)
